methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nano One Materials is a public-stage company (TSX: LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised approximately C$100M. The comparable universe for valuation consists of 1 public peer and 8 private-stage peers operating in adjacent frontier technology and energy domains, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

A peer-median triangulation approach is not applicable to this analysis. The Climate & Energy sector contains 19 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any narrow peer category—below the minimum threshold required for robust median analysis. An all-sector median would conflate fundamentally different risk and growth profiles and would therefore be misleading.

The compressed peer set and heterogeneity of business models across the private comparables limit the applicability of traditional multiples-based approaches. Valuation of Nano One would require either: (1) a detailed discounted cash flow analysis calibrated to the company's technology commercialization timeline and capital intensity, (2) reliance on the single public comparable (LNZA itself), or (3) a structured scenario analysis anchored to capital requirements and exit multiples observed in the broader frontier-tech M&A market. Key uncertainties include technology maturation risk, competitive positioning relative to stated private peers, and the path to positive unit economics.
